microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's recovery validation program from a **2-person operation** into a platformized delivery system, standardizing cross-functional workflows to sustain 45+ annual resilience drills and drive **$14M+ enterprise impact** across Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led **end-to-end program delivery** of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining requirements and self-service scheduling capabilities that reduced operational toil by 30% and transitioned execution to a scalable, repeatable model.

Directed 14 high-stakes cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility — including serving as **bridge lead** for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract — sequencing dependencies and ensuring zero stakeholder surprises at handoffs.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability, delivering a rack-level drill program in **4 months** at a 94% recovery rate, surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a new model for continuous enterprise validation.
Built an internal **AI agent** for drill planning and restructured program workflows, cutting planning cycle time by 39% and increasing drill capacity by 21% without adding headcount.
microsoft_2023: Drove **cross-functional adoption** of AI-powered code generation workflows, conducting demos and training across 14 Azure service teams, saving 37 engineering hours monthly and building a repeatable enablement playbook.

Translated user research into roadmap action — facilitated **11+ discovery interviews** with Azure engineering teams to surface critical feature gaps, directly influencing the product roadmap to include intent-based YAML generation.
Improved program-wide data integrity by migrating documentation to an **AI-powered semantic search** platform with rigorous metadata standards, cutting lookup time by 83% across service teams.
microsoft_2022: Secured cross-functional alignment across **140+ teams** on a unified automation prioritization framework, accelerating region launches by 28% and generating $3M in pull-forward revenue.
Ran structured discovery with 20+ service teams to map **81 hours of per-region manual toil**, translating findings into an executable roadmap that sequenced automation across critical launch paths.
Built a **Power BI dashboard** tracking operational toil across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to pinpoint automation gaps and direct resourcing toward highest-impact workstreams.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by defining the legacy OS migration strategy, mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations, and sequencing workstreams to prevent blockers at handoffs.
Drove alignment across IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and reducing cross-team coordination friction.
Facilitated Agile ceremonies — sprint planning, retrospectives, backlog grooming — resolving **40+ high-priority tickets** and stabilizing the developer intake process across concurrent workstreams.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by owning the end-to-end scoping and invoicing lifecycle for 200+ monthly proposals, redesigning the CRM process to eliminate handoff gaps and accelerate close cycles.

Reduced Customer Acquisition Cost by **13%** and lifted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing web presence and Google profile to align with target customer segments.

Conducted profitability analysis across **1,000+ project records** to identify margin leaks, delivering pricing model recommendations that improved leadership's ability to scope and prioritize high-value accounts.

tailoring_notes: 'End-to-end delivery' and 'sequencing dependencies' from JD woven into microsoft_ft bullets 2 and 3 to mirror TPM delivery language exactly.
'Surfacing blockers / no one caught off-guard at handoffs' mirrored in microsoft_ft bullet 3 and amazon_robotics bullet 1 using JD's own framing.
'Design-partner / strategic-deal tier where requirements evolve' echoed in microsoft_ft bullet 1 via sovereign-cloud contract context and enterprise customer scope.
'Drive programs without authority' / 'influence' reflected in microsoft_2022 bullet 1 ('secured cross-functional alignment across 140+ teams') and amazon_robotics bullet 2.
'AI agent' and 'LLM-powered tools' (JD nice-to-have) surfaced explicitly in microsoft_ft bullet 5 and microsoft_2023 bullet 3 to hit the conversational AI fluency signal.
